pi_items.source: Web search confirmed FDA, EMA, and international guidelines contraindicate metformin at eGFR < 30 mL/min/1.73 m² due to lactic acidosis risk

privileged_info_summary: Four French patients in MedinTux with documented medication safety issues. MARTIN Sophie (DOB 1985-03-22) has dual RAAS blockade with Ramipril and Valsartan (ACC/AHA Class III contraindication). BERNARD Pierre (DOB 1968-11-30) has documented NSAID allergy but Ibuprofene 400mg prescribed. MOREAU Francois (DOB 1955-12-01) has duplicate beta-blockers (Atenolol 50mg and Bisoprolol 5mg) with risk of additive bradycardia. LEROY Isabelle (DOB 1979-04-15) has Metformin 850mg prescribed despite DFG 22 ml/min (absolute contraindication per FDA/EMA guidelines). All four clinical scenarios are medically accurate and represent real polypharmacy safety risks. Task requires creating corrected prescriptions and consultation notes for each patient; pass threshold is 60 points (minimum 3 prescriptions corrected).
